ops.title: Fail loud on missing hub commits to preserve provenance
ops.body: A Hub push that returns None for its commit must crash explicitly, not silently lose provenance tracking. Discovered debugging the C5 export chain: `cc_steer/export.py` threads the returned hub commit into the provenance record, so a silent None propagates as a dataset revision with no traceable source commit — the failure only surfaces much later when a join against the provenance ledger comes up empty. Fixed in fca03148963c ("fix(export): fail loud when a hub push returns no commit") after a Linux-ty diagnostic flagged the optional flow; macOS ty missed it (see the ty-on-macOS-is-blind memory).
